zotero.abstractNote: How do humans learn and remember in a world that is both dynamic and structured? While traditional models of learning and memory often treat these processes as separate, recent work suggests they are deeply intertwined. Here, we propose a framework that integrates these two perspectives, suggesting that learning and memory are dynamic processes that rely on the structure of the environment. We test this framework using a series of behavioral experiments and computational models, showing that it can account for a wide range of phenomena in human learning and memory.
